insights.claim: The population-weighted U.S. average for stroke is 3.51%.
insights.method_note: Population-weighted mean across ZCTAs with estimates.

insights.claim: The most-deprived ADI tenth averages 2.8 points higher than the least-deprived tenth.
insights.method_note: Population-weighted ADI national-rank decile means; ecological, not causal.

insights.claim: Across ZCTAs, stroke is most associated with area deprivation index rank (Spearman rho=0.704, n=32,193).
insights.method_note: Spearman rank correlation; place-level association only.

insights.claim: Highest-burden ZCTA: 90073 (Los Angeles County, CA) at 15.7%.
insights.method_note: Maximum ZCTA value; ties broken by population.

insights.claim: About 16.3 million people live in ZCTAs at or above the high-burden threshold (5.3%).
insights.method_note: Population in ZCTAs at or above the 90th percentile (5.3%).
